name: Tortilla Espanola (Potato Omelette, Spanish S
recipeInstructions:
1. Leaving the skins on, slice the new potatoes thinly.
2. Peel, split, and slice onions. In a large saute pan, heat olive oil (the best you can afford). You need at least 1/4 inch of oil in your pan.
3. Saute potatoes and onions with garlic over low flame until the potatoes are soft and the onions are translucent. Remove mixture with a slotted spoon and place into a greased round cake pan, spring form pan, cast iron skillet or terra-cotta baking dish. If using something small, make two portions.
4. Season eggs and pour over potato mixture. Bake at 350F for 20 minutes.
5. Test the tortilla by inserting a knife in the center. When it is set and the top is slightly browned, remove from oven.
6. Let cool for 5 minutes, run a knife around the outside, invert onto a round platter, and serve at room temperature.
Buffet menu: Torta Rustica filled with spinach and red peppers, cheese, and ham
This potato omelette pairs well with a platter of Italian sausages grilled outdoors on the Weber grill. It also complements breads, muffins, bagels, muffins, donuts, etc.
